2605.06289.title: Multimodal Deep Generative Model for Semi-Supervised Learning under Class Imbalance
2605.06289.paper_summary: When modeling class-imbalanced data, it is crucial to address the imbalance, as models trained on such data tend to be biased towards the majority classes. This problem is amplified under partial supervision, where pseudo-labels for unlabeled data are predicted based on imbalanced labeled data, propagating the bias. While recent semi-supervised models address class imbalance, they typically assume single-modal input data. However, with the growing availability of multimodal data, it is essential to leverage complementary modalities. In this article, we propose a multimodal deep generative model for semi-supervised learning under class imbalance. Our approach uses separate encoders for each modality, sharing latent variables across modalities, and simplifies joint posterior computation with a product-of-experts method. To further address class imbalance, we replace typical Gaussian distributions with Student's t-distributions for the prior, encoder, and decoder, better capturing the heavy-tailed latent distributions in imbalanced data. We derive a new objective function for training the proposed model on both labeled and unlabeled data using $γ$-power divergence. Empirical results on benchmark and real-world datasets demonstrate that our model outperforms baseline methods in generalization, achieving superior classification performance for partially labeled multimodal data with imbalanced class distributions.
2605.06289.paper_summary_zh: 當建模類別不平衡的數據時，解決不平衡問題至關重要，因為在這類數據上訓練的模型往往會對主要類別產生偏見。這一問題在部分監督下被放大，因為對未標記數據的偽標籤是基於不平衡的標記數據預測的，進一步傳播了偏見。雖然最近的半監督模型解決了類別不平衡問題，但它們通常假設單一模態的輸入數據。然而，隨著多模態數據的日益可用，利用互補模態變得至關重要。在本文中，我們提出了一種針對類別不平衡的半監督學習的多模態深度生成模型。我們的方法為每個模態使用單獨的編碼器，並在模態之間共享潛在變量，並通過專家乘積方法簡化聯合後驗計算。為了進一步解決類別不平衡問題，我們用斯圖登特t分佈替換了典型的高斯分佈作為先驗、編碼器和解碼器，更好地捕捉不平衡數據中的重尾潛在分佈。我們推導出一個新的目標函數，用於使用$γ$-power divergence在標記和未標記數據上訓練所提出的模型。在基準和真實世界數據集上的實證結果表明，我們的模型在泛化能力上超越了基線方法，對於具有不平衡類別分佈的部分標記多模態數據實現了優越的分類性能。
2605.06289.author: Heegeon Yoon et.al.
